narration: Last time we built the difference quotient. Now let's see what happens as h goes to zero.
The secant line becomes the tangent line. That's the key.
This gives us the definition of the derivative. F prime of x equals the limit as h approaches zero of the quantity f of x plus h minus f of x, all over h.
Let's prove it works. Take f of x equals x squared.
Plug it in. We get the limit as h goes to zero of the quantity x plus h, squared, minus x squared, all over h.
Now expand the quantity x plus h, squared. That's x plus h times x plus h. FOIL it out: x times x, x times h, h times x, h times h. That gives us x squared plus 2xh plus h squared.
Substitute that back in. The x squareds cancel, leaving 2xh plus h squared, all over h.
Factor out the h. The h's cancel.
Now plug in h equals zero. 2x plus zero is just 2x.
Let's check. F prime of zero equals 2 times zero, which is zero. That's exactly what we saw, flat at the bottom. F prime of 2 equals 2 times 2, which is 4. The slope was 4. It works.
You just computed your first derivative, straight from the definition.
